Section Title: Granola stops at notes. Notion is where work moves forward. > FAQs
Content:
How does Notion compare to Granola for meeting notes?
Notion provides transcriptions, AI-driven summaries, and direct integration into tasks, docs, and knowledge bases. Granola focuses on capturing notes but lacks deeper workspace connections. Is Notion a good alternative to Granola?
If you're looking for more than just transcription, absolutely. Notion gives you meeting notes plus a complete workspace for docs, tasks, wikis, and databases. Many teams tell us they love having everything connected instead of scattered across different apps. Is Notion more cost-efficient than Granola?
Yes. Notion’s Business plan includes AI notes plus unlimited pages, tasks, wikis, and more—often at or below what you’d pay for Granola’s single-purpose subscription. Can Notion handle enterprise compliance and security?
Section Title: Granola stops at notes. Notion is where work moves forward. > FAQs
Content:
Notion meets strict enterprise standards, with advanced permissions, data residency options, and best-in-class privacy controls. Granola’s Mac-only approach limits large-scale deployment. Why should teams switch from Granola to Notion?
Notion centralizes meeting notes, tasks, knowledge, and workflows in one place. This eliminates data silos, saves money, and boosts productivity through AI-driven automations.
Section Title: Transform meetings into action with Notion
Content:
Try Notion free and see how a truly integrated workspace unlocks better notes, better tasks, and better outcomes.

results.title: Granola vs Notion AI: Meeting Notes Comparison 2026
excerpts:
Section Title: Granola vs Notion AI: Meeting Notes Comparison 2026 > ... > Granola's Automatic Transcription
Content:
Granola joins your meetings and transcribes everything automatically. Connect your calendar, and the tool handles recording, transcription, and initial organization without manual intervention.
The workflow is hands-off:
Granola detects scheduled meetings automatically
Joins video calls and records audio
Transcribes in real-time with speaker identification
Extracts action items and key decisions
Organizes notes by meeting, participant, and topic
For recurring meetings, Granola tracks conversation threads across sessions, connecting related discussions over time.
Section Title: Granola vs Notion AI: Meeting Notes Comparison 2026 > ... > Notion AI's Manual Note-Taking
Content:
Notion AI doesn't transcribe meetings. You still take notes manually during the call, typing into a Notion page as the conversation happens. Notion AI adds capabilities to enhance those manual notes:
Summarize what you've typed
Generate action items from your notes
Expand brief notes into fuller documentation
Translate notes between languages
Extract key points from longer entries
The difference is fundamental: Granola captures the actual meeting, while Notion AI processes whatever you manage to type during it.
[Try Granola Free](https://go.granola.ai/zack-proser?utm_source=zackproser&utm_medium=blog&utm_campaign=granola-vs-notion-ai&utm_content=inline-cta)
 ... 
Section Title: Granola vs Notion AI: Meeting Notes Comparison 2026 > ... > Executive Strategy Sessions
Content:
**Granola** : Records full strategic discussion, preserves context of how decisions were reached, creates searchable archive of leadership reasoning.
**Notion AI** : You capture key strategic points in your strategy workspace, use AI to expand notes into full documentation and connect to relevant projects.

Section Title: ... > What users say about Notion Meeting Recorder and Granola
Content:
Granola's limited platform support and integration capabilities also drive users to seek more comprehensive solutions. The service currently lacks mobile apps, restricting users to desktop-only usage on Mac and Windows systems. Additionally, it offers no native integrations with popular tools like CRM systems or Slack, requiring users to manually export and share notes. For teams that need seamless workflow integration or mobile accessibility, these limitations make Granola unsuitable for their requirements, prompting them to switch to more feature-complete alternatives.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Does Granola integrate with software like Hubspot, Salesforce, or Linear?
Content:
No. Granola currently has no external integrations at launch. Like Apple Notes, you can copy and share the final notes, but there are no native CRM or other software integrations yet. Data can be exported manually, but there's no automated connection to external platforms.

results.title: Granola Integration - Attio
excerpts:
Section Title: Granola
Content:
Your AI-powered meeting notes, added to Attio
[Installapp](https://help.granola.ai/article/attio)
Official app
Section Title: Granola > Built by
Content:
Granola
 ... 
Section Title: Granola > Overview
Content:
Connect Granola and Attio to make sure your customer notes always reflect the conversations you’re having.
Section Title: Granola > How it works
Content:
Use Granola to take notes during your customer meetings.
**Add to Attio from a note**
After a meeting, click Share > Attio.
Granola will suggest which Attio record to share your note to: choose a Person, Company or Deal (or you can choose manually)
**Add to Attio from a folder**
Create a Granola folder
Go to Folder Settings > Integrations and choose which Attio records to add meeting notes to.
When a meeting ends, add the notes to your Folder. Granola will automatically send notes to the relevant Attio records.
Notes will appear automatically in Attio. No copy/pasting required.

results.title: Really cool seeing how Granola and Notion’s note-taking features are designed to compete against one another. Notion’s designs aren’t flashy but they’re exceedingly well thought out in terms of usage… | Hang 🤙 Xu | 10 comments
excerpts:
I already have all my work and notes in Notion
Notion has a far more robust platform for databases and content and my meeting notes were going to end up on Notion anyway
I didn’t want to pay for a second subscription for identical features
However, I had to think of all this on the spot, which meant I didn't previously consciously choose Notion over Granola. So I started a few meetings to figure out how the switch happened and realized it was due almost entirely to Notion's meeting notification design. Notion's notifications were:
More timely - shown a few seconds before Granola's notification. Strategically placed - Shown at the center of the screen so my eyes noticed it first. I haven't noticed Granola's notification for weeks if not months. It's like Granola completely disappeared from my attention.
 ... 
Section Title: **Transcript**
Content:
So yesterday I received an automated e-mail from Chris, the founder of Granola. If you don't know granola, it's a tool that summarizes your meeting notes. First of all, I think this is super clever. Whenever you see it used to drops off, you should send an e-mail asking for feedback as to why they stop using it. This one is really interesting to me because I didn't even realize I stopped using granola and switched over to something else until this e-mail got to me. Like it was not a conscious decision on my part. What's really cool here is just watching how Notion designed their feature to replace another existing tool out there. Now. Granola came out first and I think there. Probably are more users for granola and. As a result, Notion has to somehow supplant granola, and the way it worked is when you create a new meeting. Normally you see a notification on top right for granola, but in this case notions notification OU 1st and then granolas shows U later.
 ... 
Section Title: **Transcript**
Content:
[Shangbo (Kylin) Chen](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kylinc?trk=public_post_comment_actor-name) 4mo
My new firm uses Gong which I never heard of in b2c space. The company used Gong heavily to record all customer conversations and even company-wide meetings such as All-hands or workshops, which made it easy to share important clips.
You add a gong assistance email to the google meeting invite and it will email you a link to a video + an AI summary - which is one of the best meeting summary I've seen. I thought Notion / Granola would be miles ahead of any B2B competition but Gong changed my mind. I suppose high frequency usage scenario + higher stake (b2b sales numbers on the line) can also produce better product experience.
1 Reaction
[Trace Orozco](https://ca.linkedin.com/in/traceorozco?trk=public_post_comment_actor-name) 4mo

results.title: Top 5 AI note takers for teams: Granola, Fireflies.ai, Grain, Notion, Fathom | Justin Watt posted on the topic | LinkedIn
excerpts:
Section Title: Top 5 AI note takers for teams: Granola, Fireflies.ai, Grain, Notion, Fathom
Content:
[Fireflies.ai](https://www.linkedin.com/company/fireflies-inc?trk=public_post-text) 🥈
Used to be our # 1 until Granola stepped up their game. Still rock-solid for both personal and team use, good customization options, and they have an API for workflow integration. Just got outpaced on UX of day to day and team features. 3. [Grain](https://www.linkedin.com/company/grainhq?trk=public_post-text) 🥉
Shines for live note-taking during meetings with real-time team collaboration. Integration options are there, but customization is more limited than the top two. 4. [Notion](https://www.linkedin.com/company/notionhq?trk=public_post-text) 📝
Their desktop AI notes and summaries just went live (still in beta) but quickly becoming recommended (especially if your team already lives in Notion) as this might be your best bet for seamless collaboration.
 ...

- Polishes your writing and adapts tone instantly using GrammarlyGO. Perfect for emails, reports, and content.
 ... 
Section Title: Top 5 AI note takers for teams: Granola, Fireflies.ai, Grain, Notion, Fathom > More Relevant Posts
Content:
For example:
Titling the chart at the beginning of an agenda item helped keep people focused by serving as a reminder of the topic under discussion. Recording live what people said often helped speakers refine their thinking and statements as they were seeing from the flip chart recording how others were hearing their words. When disagreements occurred, recording the alternative views on the chart slowed down the conversation and allowed people, instead of arguing with each other, to focus on hearing one another and creating new alternatives. By letting people know that the flip charts were the official record of the thoughts of the group, people paid much more attention to ensuring what was being recorded accurately reflected their thoughts and feelings.

results.title: Notion - Granola Docs & Help Center
excerpts:
Section Title: Notion
Content:
Export Granola meeting notes to Notion. Create Notion pages from your meeting summaries and transcripts.
Once connected, you can send meeting notes to Notion with one click from each note. Notes are saved to a dedicated Notion database that Granola creates when you connect. **What's supported today:** sharing notes **one at a time** from the share menu in each note — there is no automatic or bulk sync.
If you’re using Granola for iPhone, you can share your notes to Notion from Granola on macOS/Windows - just complete these steps and share your notes on your computer.
Here’s how to set up Granola to save notes to Notion:
